violations.description: Spec file exceeds 300 lines, increasing cognitive load and debugging complexity.
violations.suggestion: Split by behavior slices (dispatch, webhook translation, event query determinism).
violations.code_snippet: 379-line file with mixed scenarios and helper logic

violations.description: Large multi-scenario suite combines policy-gate, rollout, webhook, and dispatch checks in one file.
violations.suggestion: Decompose into focused specs per capability and share fixtures/helpers.

violations.description: E2E automate spec exceeds 300 lines and duplicates helper patterns from API coverage.
violations.suggestion: Extract shared assertion helpers and keep each scenario under ~120 lines.

violations.description: Repeated envelope and payload assertion structures appear across files with minor variations.
violations.suggestion: Introduce reusable assertion helpers for envelope and provider-neutral checks.

violations.description: E2E suite primarily exercises API request flows, duplicating backend assertions from API specs.
violations.suggestion: Keep API contract assertions in API tests and reserve E2E for UI behavior validation.

recommendations: Refactor Epic F specs into smaller concern-based files.
Create shared assertion utilities for envelopes and provider-neutral payload checks.
Reduce API assertion duplication inside E2E suites.
summary: Maintainability is constrained by oversized files and duplicated assertion patterns across API/E2E suites.
